name: My lungs are messed up from years of chemical exposure at work in Murray. Can I still file a workers comp claim for occupational illness?
description: Yes, if it arose from employment; Utah presumes work-related on premises and covers chemical lung disease with medical proof.[1]

mainEntity.name: My lungs are messed up from years of chemical exposure at work in Murray. Can I still file a workers comp claim for occupational illness?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, if it arose from employment; Utah presumes work-related on premises and covers chemical lung disease with medical proof.[1]
acceptedAnswer.description: Yes, Utah’s Workers’ Compensation Act covers occupational illnesses like chemical-exposure lung disease if they arise out of and in the course of employment (AOE/COE test).[1] The Utah Labor Commission presumes claims are work-related if they occur on the clock or premises, shifting proof burden to employer.[1] Key factors: (1) Show medical causation linking exposure to illness; (2) Multiple exposure dates require analyzing the last injurious one; (3) Broad coverage favors access to benefits; (4) Examples include asbestos lung disease or silicosis.[1] Report immediately to employer, get medical documentation, and file with Labor Commission; consult attorney if denied.[1] Deadlines are strict, so act fast.
